itineraries.title: Essential Cambodia Circuit: Phnom Penh, Battambang and Angkor
itineraries.subtitle: The Khmer golden triangle in 9 days, between bustling capital, colonial countryside and millennia-old temples
specialties: Amok trey (steamed fish curry in banana leaf)
Lok lak (stir-fried beef with Kampot pepper)
Num banh chok (rice noodles with green curry, Cambodian breakfast)
Freshly pressed sugar cane juice from street vendors

visits.text: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda - official residence of the king, you'll discover the throne hall and the pagoda floor paved with 5,000 silver tiles (proper attire required, shoulders and knees must be covered)
National Museum of Cambodia - red sandstone building in Khmer style, housing the world's largest collection of Angkorian sculptures, including the 11th-century bronze Vishnu discovered at West Mebon

activities.text: Stroll along Sisowath Quay - 3 km promenade along the Tonle Sap River, bustling with terraces, street food vendors and joggers at sunset
Sunset cruise on the Mekong - one-hour traditional boat ride at the confluence of the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers, departing from Sisowath Quay

visits.text: Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum (S-21) - former high school converted into a prison by the Khmer Rouge between 1975 and 1979, audio guide available in multiple languages, a harrowing but essential visit to understand the country's history
Choeung Ek Memorial (Killing Fields) - execution site 15 km from the center, memorial stupa containing over 8,000 skulls, poignant 45-minute audio tour
Wat Phnom - 27-meter artificial hill founded in 1373 according to legend by Lady Penh, who gave the city its name; pagoda surrounded by a small shaded park

activities.text: Central Market (Phsar Thmey) - Art Deco hall from the 1930s shaped like a yellow dome, where you'll find jewelry, textiles and street food under architecture unique in Southeast Asia

visits.text: Colonial center of Battambang - Cambodia's second-largest city, you'll wander streets lined with Franco-Khmer houses from the 1920s, the old railway station and Phsar Nath market

activities.text: Bamboo Train (Norry) - bamboo platform propelled on old rails through rice paddies, roughly 7 km journey, a unique folkoric experience in Cambodia
Khmer cooking class - 3-hour workshop to learn how to prepare amok, lok lak and fresh spring rolls with ingredients from the local market

visits.text: Phnom Sampeau - sacred mountain 12 km from town, you climb 358 steps to reach the hilltop pagodas with panoramic views over rice paddies; below, the Killing Caves recall the Khmer Rouge executions
Wat Ek Phnom - ruined 11th-century temple, sculpted lintels depicting the churning of the ocean of milk, located 11 km north of Battambang amid rice fields

activities.text: Bat spectacle at Phnom Sampeau - around 5:30 PM, millions of bats exit the cave in a continuous black ribbon for 30 to 45 minutes, a striking natural phenomenon

visits.text: Orientation tour of Siem Reap - old French quarter, banks of the Siem Reap River, Psaar Chas (Old Market) where you'll find handicrafts, spices and souvenirs
Artisans d'Angkor - workshop-school founded in 1998 that trains young Cambodians in wood and stone carving, lacquer work and gilding; free workshop tours

activities.text: Apsara dance show in the evening - recreation of sacred Khmer dances inscribed on UNESCO's intangible heritage list since 2003, accompanied by a buffet dinner at several restaurants in Siem Reap

visits.text: Sunrise over Angkor Wat - arrive around 5 AM to watch the towers reflected in the pools; this 12th-century temple dedicated to Vishnu is the world's largest religious monument at 162 hectares

Angkor Thom and Bayon - fortified city spanning 9 km², you enter through the South Gate flanked by 108 statues - 54 gods and 54 demons holding a naga; the Bayon features 216 monumental faces carved in stone, dating from the reign of Jayavarman VII in the 13th century
Ta Prohm - 12th-century temple left in a semi-ruined state, giant roots of silk-cotton and strangler fig trees wrap around the galleries; made famous by the film Tomb Raider (2001)

activities.text: Tuk-tuk rides through the Angkor Archaeological Park - Small Circuit route (17 km) connecting the main temples, your driver waits for you at each stop

visits.text: Banteay Srei - nicknamed 'the citadel of women', this small 10th-century pink sandstone temple located 25 km from Angkor boasts the most finely carved bas-reliefs in all Khmer art
Preah Khan - vast monastic complex of 56 hectares built in 1191 by Jayavarman VII, labyrinthine galleries and a two-story building unique in Angkorian architecture
Terrace of Elephants and Terrace of the Leper King - royal platforms of Angkor Thom from which the king watched processions, 300-meter-long bas-reliefs depicting elephants, garudas and apsaras

activities.text: Phare Ponleu Selpak circus show - troupe born in Battambang blending acrobatics, theater, live music and Cambodian social themes; performances every evening at 8 PM under a big top in Siem Reap

visits.text: Floating village of Kampong Phluk - houses on 8 to 10-meter stilts on Tonle Sap, Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake whose surface expands from 2,500 km² in dry season to 16,000 km² during monsoon

activities.text: Boat ride through the flooded forest - rowing pirogue through the mangroves of Kampong Phluk, you glide beneath the submerged canopy during rainy season (October-March)

APOPO Visitor Center - demining center where African giant pouched rats are trained to detect landmines, 45-minute educational visit showing the decontamination work in Cambodia, one of the world's most heavily mined countries

visits.text: Russian Market (Tuol Tom Poung) - covered market favored by expats and travelers for its textiles, antiques and street food; ideal for last-minute souvenir shopping

activities.text: Final stroll along the Mekong - sunset over the river from Sisowath Quay or from Silk Island (Koh Dach) accessible by ferry from Chroy Changvar Bridge

seo.h1_title: Essential Cambodia Circuit: Phnom Penh, Battambang and Angkor
seo.intro_paragraph: This 9-day circuit takes you through Cambodia's three unmissable highlights. You begin in Phnom Penh, a vibrant capital perched at the confluence of the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers. You continue to Battambang, a colonial town surrounded by rice paddies where the legendary Bamboo Train still rolls. You finish in Siem Reap and the temples of Angkor, an architectural marvel rising from the jungle. A compact journey that delivers the essence of the country in a single trip.
seo.seo_hook: Carved stones emerging from jungle, golden rice paddies stretching to infinity, and smiles everywhere. Essential Cambodia unfolds in three unforgettable stages.
featured_list: Phnom Penh: the capital pulses between Mekong waters and living memory.
Battambang: the Khmer countryside preserves its colonial charm.
Angkor: temples emerge from the jungle at sunrise.
Tonle Sap: the giant lake dictates the rhythm of floating villages.

places_taglines.KH::phnom-penh: Where the river's pulse meets palace grandeur and bustling markets.
places_taglines.KH::battambang: Colonial facades frame endless rice paddies beneath vast skies.
places_taglines.KH::siem-reap: Gateway to ancient temples and modern nightlife intertwined.

practical_context.visa: E-visa online (36 USD) or visa on arrival (30 USD + photo), valid for 30 days
practical_context.currency: Cambodian riel (KHR) but US dollars accepted everywhere; small USD bills are essential
practical_context.health: No mandatory vaccines but hepatitis A, typhoid and rabies recommended; antimalarial treatment advised outside Phnom Penh and Siem Reap
practical_context.transport: Tuk-tuk in cities, car with driver between towns, air-conditioned buses (Giant Ibis, Mekong Express)

itineraries.title: Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
itineraries.subtitle: The complete 14-day circuit from the capital to the temples, passing through Mekong dolphins, Battambang countryside, and Kampot beaches
specialties: Amok trey (steamed fish curry in banana leaf)
Lok lak (beef stir-fried with Kampot pepper)
Num banh chok (rice noodles with curry, the national breakfast)
Kampot pepper (PGI since 2010, red, black, or white)
Kuy teav (morning noodle soup, a Phnom Penh staple)
Tuk trey (fermented fish sauce, a universal condiment)

visits.text: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda - residence of the king, you'll discover the throne hall, gardens, and the pagoda's floor paved with 5,000 silver tiles (appropriate dress required)
National Museum of Cambodia - red sandstone building from 1920, world's largest collection of Angkorian sculptures, the 11th-century bronze Vishnu is the centerpiece

activities.text: Sunset cruise on the Mekong - one-hour traditional boat ride at the confluence of the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers, departing from Sisowath Quay
Evening stroll along Sisowath Quay and street food at the night market - skewers, Cambodian crepes, and freshly pressed sugarcane juice

visits.text: Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum (S-21) - former high school converted into a detention center by the Khmer Rouge (1975-1979), audio guide available in English, a harrowing but essential visit
Choeung Ek Memorial (Killing Fields) - execution site 15 km from the center, memorial stupa containing over 8,000 skulls, 45-minute audio tour
Wat Phnom - 27-meter artificial hill founded in 1373, pagoda surrounded by a shaded park at the heart of the city

activities.text: Central Market (Phsar Thmey) - Art Deco hall from the 1930s shaped like a yellow dome, jewelry, textiles, and street food

visits.text: Stop in Skun ('Spiderville') - this town's market is famous for its fried spiders served as snacks; you can observe (or taste) the crispy tarantulas
Colonial town of Kratie - small town on the banks of the Mekong, French houses from the 1920s, peaceful riverside and lively morning market

activities.text: Boat excursion to Kampi to observe Irrawaddy dolphins - population of about 90 individuals in this stretch of the Mekong, best viewing at sunset when they surface to breathe

visits.text: Koh Trong Island - local ferry to cross the Mekong (5 minutes), stilt village, pomelo orchards and vegetable gardens, end-of-the-world atmosphere

activities.text: Bicycle loop around the island (≈ 9 km) - flat track along the Mekong, you'll pass water buffaloes, playing children, and rice paddies, with a possible stop at a local homestay

visits.text: Sambor Prei Kuk - pre-Angkorian site listed as UNESCO World Heritage in 2017, former capital of the Chenla Empire (6th-7th century), brick temples hidden in the forest halfway between Kratie and Siem Reap
Arrival in Siem Reap and orientation tour - riverbanks, old French quarter, Psaar Chas (Old Market)

activities.text: Apsara dance show in the evening - recreation of sacred Khmer dances inscribed on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list since 2003

visits.text: Sunrise at Angkor Wat - arrival around 5 am, reflection of the five towers in the basins; 12th-century temple dedicated to Vishnu, the world's largest religious monument (162 hectares)

Angkor Thom and Bayon - fortified city, South Gate lined with 108 statues - 54 gods and 54 demons holding a naga, the Bayon features 216 monumental carved faces from the reign of Jayavarman VII (13th century)
Ta Prohm - 12th-century temple in semi-ruined state, giant silk-cotton tree roots sprawling over the galleries, filming location for Tomb Raider (2001)

activities.text: Small Circuit by tuk-tuk (17 km) - connects the main temples, your driver waits at each stop

visits.text: Banteay Srei - 10th-century pink sandstone temple 25 km from Angkor, the most finely carved bas-reliefs in Khmer art, nicknamed 'the citadel of women'
Beng Mealea - collapsed 12th-century temple deep in the jungle 68 km from Siem Reap, completely unrestored, you'll climb over stone blocks engulfed by vegetation
Roluos Group (Bakong, Preah Ko) - first capital of the Khmer Empire in the 9th century, brick and sandstone temples 13 km from Siem Reap, far from the crowds

activities.text: Phare Ponleu Selpak circus show - troupe originally from Battambang combining acrobatics, live music, and Cambodian social themes, under the big top at 8 pm

visits.text: Kampong Phluk floating village - houses on 8- to 10-meter stilts on Tonle Sap Lake, flooded forest explored by canoe
Prek Toal Bird Sanctuary - one of Southeast Asia's most important waterbird sites, colonies of spot-billed pelicans, greater adjutants, and painted storks

activities.text: Traditional boat journey on Tonle Sap - navigation among floating houses, floating schools, and fishermen with nets

visits.text: Colonial center of Battambang - the country's second-largest city, streets lined with Franco-Khmer houses from the 1920s, old railway station, and Phsar Nath market

activities.text: Bamboo Train (Norry) - bamboo platform propelled along old rails through rice paddies, journey of about 7 km
Bat spectacle at Phnom Sampeau - around 5:30 pm, millions of bats emerge from the cave in a continuous black ribbon for 30 to 45 minutes

to_next_leg.road_type: National Route 5 then National Route 4 via Phnom Penh

visits.text: Kampot town center - French colonial architecture from the 1920s along the Prek Kampot River, old French bridge, giant Durian roundabout, laid-back atmosphere
La Plantation - Kampot pepper farm (PGI since 2010), guided tour of the plots and tasting of fresh black, red, and white peppercorns on the vine

activities.text: Kayaking on Prek Kampot River (Green Cathedral) - 3 km loop through a natural mangrove canopy, calm water and filtered light

visits.text: Bokor Mountain (Preah Monivong National Park) - hill station at 1,080 meters built by the French in 1922, you'll discover the abandoned palace, ruined church, and Lok Yeay Mao pagoda with views over the Gulf of Thailand
Phnom Chhnork Cave - 7th-century Hindu temple nestled in a natural cave, you descend 203 steps to discover a brick sanctuary dedicated to Shiva

activities.text: Sunset on Kampot River - settle into one of the floating bar-restaurants to watch the sun set behind the Bokor Mountains

visits.text: Kep Crab Market - seaside market where fishermen sell live blue crab, you can savor it stir-fried with Kampot green peppercorns in adjacent restaurants
Kep National Park - 8 km hiking trail through tropical forest on the hill overlooking the sea, views of the Gulf of Thailand and Phú Quốc Island (Vietnam)

activities.text: Boat excursion to Koh Tonsay (Rabbit Island) - island 25 minutes from Kep, sandy beach lined with coconut palms, hammocks and grilled fish lunch with your feet in the sand

visits.text: SOSORO Money Museum - modern museum tracing Cambodia's economic history through its currencies, from barter to current banknotes, free admission
Russian Market (Tuol Tom Poung) - covered market favored by expats for textiles, antiques, and street food, last-minute souvenir shopping

activities.text: Architectural walk - walking circuit past the central post office, 1932 railway station, Vann Molyvann's Olympic Stadium (Khmer brutalist architecture from the 1960s), and the Independence Monument

visits.text: Silk Island (Koh Dach) - island on the Mekong accessible by ferry from Chroy Changvar Bridge, traditional silk weaving workshops, weaving villages, and river beaches

activities.text: Final stroll along Sisowath Quay - sunset over the Mekong, street food, and the feeling of a journey coming full circle

seo.h1_title: Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
seo.intro_paragraph: This 14-day grand tour traces a complete loop through Cambodia. You'll start from Phnom Penh, journey up the Mekong to Kratie to observe Irrawaddy dolphins, then head to Angkor's temples via the pre-Angkorian site of Sambor Prei Kuk. After four days in the archaeological park, you'll stop in Battambang for its colonial charm and Bamboo Train, then descend south to discover Kampot's pepper plantations, Kep's crabs, and Bokor Mountain. An itinerary that reveals every facet of the country.
seo.seo_hook: From the Mekong to lost temples, from golden rice paddies to the coves of the Gulf of Thailand. Cambodia reveals itself entirely in a 14-day loop.
featured_list: Phnom Penh: the capital pulses between Mekong waters and living memory.
Kratie: Irrawaddy dolphins surface in the river at twilight.
Angkor: temples emerge from the jungle at sunrise.
Battambang: the Bamboo Train rattles through endless rice fields.
Kampot: pepper vines climb hillsides overlooking the gulf.

places_taglines.KH::phnom-penh: Where the Mekong flows past royal palaces and bustling markets
places_taglines.KH::kratie: Home to the river's last freshwater dolphins
places_taglines.KH::siem-reap: Gateway to ancient temples and vibrant nightlife
places_taglines.KH::battambang: Colonial elegance stretched across endless rice plains
places_taglines.KH::kampot: Where mountain rivers meet pepper-scented breezes
places_taglines.KH::kep: A crab market opening onto turquoise waters

practical_context.visa: E-visa online ($36 USD) or visa on arrival ($30 USD + photo), valid for 30 days
practical_context.currency: Cambodian Riel (KHR) but US dollars are accepted everywhere; small USD bills are essential
practical_context.health: No mandatory vaccines but hepatitis A, typhoid, and rabies recommended; antimalarial treatment advised outside Phnom Penh and Siem Reap
practical_context.transport: Car with driver recommended for this grand circuit, air-conditioned buses (Giant Ibis, Mekong Express) between major cities

itineraries.title: Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
itineraries.subtitle: 12 days of adventure among Mondulkiri elephants, Ratanakiri's volcanic lake, Mekong dolphins and pristine islands
itineraries.notes: The circuit ends at Koh Rong Samloem. For the return, ferry to Sihanoukville (45 min) then road or domestic flight to Phnom Penh (4h by road or 45 min by flight). Allow an extra half-day for the return journey.
specialties: Bunong cuisine based on sticky rice and wood-fire grilled meats
Mondulkiri coffee (organically grown by Phnong minorities)
Traditional rice wine from Tampuen minorities (Ratanakiri)
Fresh Kampot pepper on the vine (PGI since 2010)
Grilled island fish served in banana leaves

visits.text: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda - swift tour of the royal residence and the floor paved with 5,000 silver tiles (modest dress required)
Central Market (Phsar Thmey) - Art Deco hall from the 1930s with its iconic yellow dome, jewelry, textiles and final preparations before heading east into the remote provinces

activities.text: Dinner along Sisowath Quay - last city meal before leaving the capital for the remote northeastern provinces, street food and optional river cruise

visits.text: Arrival in Sen Monorom - capital of Mondulkiri perched at 800 meters altitude, nicknamed 'the Switzerland of Cambodia' for its rolling green hills, fresh air and peaceful atmosphere
Doh Kromom Mountain - two sacred hills for the Bunong people (Grandfather Hill and Grandmother Hill), panoramic viewpoint over the Mondulkiri highlands, perfect at sunset

activities.text: Stroll through Sen Monorom - main street lined with guesthouses and restaurants, two lakes in the town center, local market where women from different ethnic minorities come to sell their produce

visits.text: Elephant Valley Project - ethical sanctuary run by an NGO, full day observing semi-wild elephants in the forest (no riding), you accompany Bunong mahouts and walk through the jungle alongside the pachyderms

activities.text: Encounter with a Bunong village - the Bunong (or Phnong) make up half the population of Mondulkiri, you discover their thatched-roof houses, traditional weaving and fermented rice wine

visits.text: Bousra Waterfall - the most impressive in Mondulkiri, 35 km from Sen Monorom, double cascade plunging 30 meters into the jungle, natural swimming pool at the base of the falls
Dak Dam Waterfall - on the same road as Bousra, setting of red earth and verdant hills, natural pools for picnicking and swimming

activities.text: Visit to a Mondulkiri coffee plantation - coffee is organically grown by Phnong minorities using traditional methods supported by CEDAC, you follow the process from tree to cup and taste on site

to_next_leg.road_type: Road linking Sen Monorom to Banlung (recently paved)

visits.text: Arrival in Banlung - 'the red town' due to the laterite soil that stains everything, capital of Ratanakiri nicknamed 'the Khmer Far West', end-of-the-world atmosphere

Yeak Laom volcanic lake - 700,000-year-old crater, circular lake 800 meters in diameter and 48 meters deep with crystal-clear waters, considered sacred by the Tampuen tribes; 2.5 km hiking trail around the lake, swimming allowed

activities.text: Yeak Laom Lake Cultural Museum - small museum presenting the crafts of Ratanakiri tribes (musical instruments, baskets, hand-woven textiles), direct purchase from local artisans

visits.text: Kachang Waterfall - 6 km from Banlung, waterfall in dense forest creating a veil of mist, peaceful pool at the base for swimming
Ka Tieng Waterfall - near Kachang, small cascade surrounded by large boulders, easy access by scooter on red dirt track
Banlung Market (minorities market) - every morning villagers from different ethnic groups (Tampuen, Braos, Kreung) come to sell fresh produce and traditional crafts

activities.text: Sunset from Wat Rahtanharahm - pagoda on the hill overlooking Banlung with reclining Buddha, panorama over the red lands and forest

visits.text: Ethnic villages of Ratanakiri - visit a Tampuen or Kreung village accessible by motorbike from Banlung, traditional houses, totemic cemeteries and timeless life in the forest

activities.text: Navigation on the Sesan River - motorboat excursion on the river bordered by tropical forest, stops in riverside villages inaccessible by road

to_next_leg.road_type: Road to Stung Treng then National Road 7 to Kratie

visits.text: Colonial town of Kratie - peaceful Mekong waterfront, colonial houses from the 1920s and lively local market in the morning
Koh Trong Island - local ferry across the Mekong, stilt village, pomelo orchards and vegetable gardens in an end-of-the-world atmosphere

activities.text: Irrawaddy dolphin watching at Kampi - boat trip at sunset, approximately 90 freshwater dolphins live in this stretch of the Mekong, surfacing to breathe

to_next_leg.road_type: National Road 7 then National Road 4 towards the coast

visits.text: Kampot town center - French colonial architecture along the Prek Kampot river, old bridge, Giant Durian roundabout, relaxed and bohemian atmosphere
La Plantation - Kampot pepper farm (PGI since 2010), guided tour and tasting of fresh black, red and white pepper

activities.text: Kayaking the Green Cathedral - 3 km loop under a natural canopy of mangroves on the Kampot river, calm water and filtered light

visits.text: Bokor Mountain - highland station at 1,080 m, abandoned palace from the 1920s, church ruins and Lok Yeay Mao pagoda with views over the Gulf of Thailand
Kep Crab Market - live blue crab caught in the morning, you taste it stir-fried with Kampot green pepper in restaurants by the sea

activities.text: Kep National Park - 8 km trail through tropical forest overlooking the sea, views over the gulf and Phú Quốc island in Vietnam

to_next_leg.road_type: National Road 3 towards Sihanoukville then ferry

visits.text: Koh Rong Samloem - more preserved island and less party-oriented than its neighbor Koh Rong, 45-minute ferry from Sihanoukville; Saracen Bay offers a crescent of white sand beach lined with coconut palms

activities.text: Snorkeling from the beach - coral reefs accessible by swimming around the island, tropical fish and sea turtles (with luck)
Night swimming with bioluminescent plankton - natural phenomenon visible on moonless nights, every movement in the water creates fluorescent blue sparkles

visits.text: Sunset Beach - isolated beach on the island's west coast, accessible only on foot via a 30-minute jungle trail or by boat, sunsets facing the open sea

activities.text: Trek through the island's inland jungle - trail crossing the tropical forest connecting Saracen Bay to Sunset Beach, endemic wildlife (geckos, butterflies, non-venomous snakes)
Sea kayaking along the coastline - exploring coves, cliffs and deserted beaches accessible only by sea

seo.h1_title: Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
seo.intro_paragraph: This 12-day circuit takes you into the Cambodia most travelers never see. You leave Phnom Penh for the hills of Mondulkiri, nicknamed the Switzerland of Cambodia, where you walk with elephants through the jungle and visit Bunong villages. You continue to Ratanakiri and its 700,000-year-old volcanic lake Yeak Laom, surrounded by tribes living outside of time. After a stop on the Mekong at Kratie for the Irrawaddy dolphins, you descend to the pepper plantations of Kampot and finish on the preserved beaches of Koh Rong Samloem. A journey for those who want to see the other face of Cambodia.
seo.seo_hook: Elephants in the mist, a sacred lake at the bottom of a crater, red earth stretching endlessly. Cambodia hides its greatest secrets far from the temples.
featured_list: Mondulkiri: elephants walk freely through the jungle.
Ratanakiri: the volcanic lake has held its crystal waters for 700,000 years.
Kratie: freshwater dolphins swim up the Mekong at twilight.
Kampot: pepper grows on hillsides facing the gulf.
Koh Rong Samloem: plankton lights up the sea on moonless nights.

places_taglines.KH::phnom-penh: Where the capital launches your journey into wild country
places_taglines.KH::sen-monorom: Where highland mists unveil the Bunong world
places_taglines.KH::banlung: Where red earth guards the sacred lake of the tribes
places_taglines.KH::kratie: Where the last freshwater dolphins still surface at dusk
places_taglines.KH::kampot: Where rivers wind between mountains and pepper vines
places_taglines.KH::koh-rong-samloem: Where plankton paints the waves with stars on moonless nights

practical_context.visa: E-visa online (36 USD) or visa on arrival (30 USD + photo), valid 30 days
practical_context.currency: Cambodian Riel (KHR) and US dollar; in Mondulkiri and Ratanakiri, ATMs are scarce - bring USD cash
practical_context.budget_per_day_usd: 35-65 USD (basic to mid-range accommodation, local meals, transport)
practical_context.health: Antimalarial treatment strongly recommended for Mondulkiri and Ratanakiri; hepatitis A, typhoid and rabies vaccines advised; mosquito repellent essential
practical_context.transport: Minivan between cities (Virak Buntham, Sen Monorom Express), scooter rentals available locally in Banlung and Sen Monorom (7-8 USD/day), car with driver recommended for comfort on secondary roads
